blocks:
Sūrat At-Taḥrīm (The Prohibition)

Medinan, its number of verses is twelve verses by consensus (بالإجماع)* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ ما أَحَلَّ اللهُ لَكَ تَبْتَغِي مَرْضاتَ أَزْواجِكَ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. (1) O Prophet! Why do you prohibit what Allah has made lawful for you, seeking to please your wives? And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) who said: ‘A’ishah and Hafsah became aware of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) while He was with Mariyah. So the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny) said: "By Allah, I will not approach her after this." So Allah commanded Him to make expiation (يُكَفِّر) for His oath.

And it was narrated: That He was alone with Mariyah on the day belonging to Hafsah or ‘A’ishah. So Hafsah became aware of that and reproached Him concerning it. So He prohibited Mariyah, and [the verse] was revealed.

And it was said: He drank honey at Hafsah’s house. So ‘A’ishah, Sawdah, and Safiyyah agreed [to a plan] and said to Him: "We perceive from you the smell of al-maghafir (المغافير)." So He prohibited the honey, and [the verse] was revealed. And the complete discussion concerning it will follow.

(٢) قَدْ فَرَضَ اللهُ لَكُمْ تَحِلَّةَ أَيْمانِكُمْ ** (2) Allah has certainly prescribed for you the absolution of your oaths. He has legislated for you its absolution (تحليلها), and that is the dissolution (حلّ) of what you have bound by the expiation (بالكفارة). وَاللهُ مَوْلاكُمْ (and Allah is your Master), the Manager of your affairs (متولي أموركم). وَهُوَ الْعَلِيمُ (and He is the All-knowing) of what is beneficial for you (بما يصلحكم), الْحَكِيمُ (the All-wise), the Perfecter (المتقن) in His actions (أفعاله) and His judgments (وأحكامه).

(٣) وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلى بَعْضِ أَزْواجِهِ حَدِيثاً فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَتْ بِهِ ** (3) And when the Prophet confided a matter to one of his wives, and when she divulged it**

—meaning she informed of it—وَأَظْهَرَهُ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ (and Allah disclosed it to him)—meaning Allah informed the Prophet of the matter, that is, of its divulgence—عَرَّفَ بَعْضَهُ (He made known part of it)—meaning the Messenger made known part of what she had done—وَأَعْرَضَ عَنْ بَعْضٍ (and disregarded part)—meaning He refrained from informing her of part of it, out of generosity (تكرّماً).

It was read with takhfif (lightening the bāʾ) in al-Majmaʿ. Abu Bakr ibn Abi Ayyash chose the takhfif, and this is one of the ten letters (الحروف العشرة) which he said: "I included them in the recitation (قراءة) of Asim from the recitation (قراءة) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), until his recitation (قراءته) was purified," meaning the recitation (قراءة) of Ali (peace be upon him).

فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَها بِهِ قالَتْ مَنْ أَنْبَأَكَ هذا قالَ نَبَّأَنِيَ الْعَلِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ ** when He informed her about it, she said, 'Who informed you of this?' He said, 'The All-knowing, the All-aware informed me.'**

Al-Qummi (narrates): The reason for its revelation was that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in one of the houses of his wives, and Mariyah the Copt (مارية القبطية) was with Him, serving Him. One day, He was in the house of Hafsah, and Hafsah went out for a need of hers. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then engaged with Mariyah. Hafsah learned of this and became angry. She approached the Messenger of Allah and said: "O Messenger of Allah, on my day, in my house, and on my bed?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم felt ashamed before her and said: "Enough! I have forbidden Mariyah upon myself, and I will never approach her after this. And I shall confide a secret to you: if you inform anyone of it, then upon you is the curse of Allah, the angels, and all mankind." She said: "Yes, what is it?" He said: "Indeed, Abu Bakr will assume the Caliphate (الخلافة) after me, and then after him, your father." قالَتْ مَنْ أَنْبَأَكَ هذا قالَ نَبَّأَنِيَ الْعَلِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ (She said, 'Who informed you of this?' He said, 'The All-knowing, the All-aware informed me.') Hafsah informed Aishah of this on that very day, and Aishah informed Abu Bakr. Abu Bakr came to Umar and said to him: "Aishah informed me of something from Hafsah, and I do not trust her word, so you ask Hafsah." Umar came to Hafsah and said: "What is this that Aishah informed about you?" She denied it and said: "I did not say anything of that to her." Umar said to her: "Indeed, this is true, so inform us so that we may proceed with it." She said: "Yes, the Messenger of Allah has indeed said it." So, four people gathered to poison (يسمّوا) the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then Gabriel descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with this Surah.

He said: وَأَظْهَرَهُ اللهُ عَلَيْهِ ** (and Allah disclosed it to him)—meaning Allah disclosed to Him what she had informed of, and what they intended (همّوا به) regarding His killing. عَرَّفَ بَعْضَهُ ** (He made known part of it)—meaning He informed her and said: "Why did you inform of what I informed you?" وَأَعْرَضَ عَنْ بَعْضٍ ** (and disregarded part)—He said: He did not inform them of what He knew regarding their intention (همّوا به) to kill Him.

And in al-Majmaʿ: It is said that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was alone one day with His slave-girl Umm Ibrahim, Mariyah the Copt (مارية القبطيّة), for Aishah. Hafsah became aware of this. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Do not inform Aishah of this," and He forbade Mariyah upon Himself. Hafsah then informed Aishah of the news and asked her to keep it secret. Allah informed His Prophet of this, and that is His saying: وَإِذْ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلى بَعْضِ أَزْواجِهِ حَدِيثاً (And when the Prophet confided a matter to one of his wives)—meaning Hafsah. And when He forbade Mariyah the Copt (مارية القبطيّة) upon Himself, He informed Hafsah that Abu Bakr and Umar would rule after Him. So, He made known to her part of the news that she had divulged. **وَأَعْرَضَ عَنْ بَعْضٍ ** (and disregarded part)—that Abu Bakr and Umar would rule after me.

He said: And similar to that is what al-Ayyashi narrated from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him), except that he added to it: that each one of them informed her father of that, so He reproached them concerning the matter of Mariyah and what they had divulged concerning it, and He disregarded reproaching them concerning the other matter.

(٤) إِنْ تَتُوبا إِلَى اللهِ ** (4) If you two repent to Allah, This is an address to Hafsah and 'A'ishah, using the shift in address (الالتفات) for emphasis in the admonishment. فَقَدْ صَغَتْ قُلُوبُكُما ** (your hearts have certainly swerved) For there has been found in you both that which necessitates repentance, which is the swerving (ميل) of your hearts from what is obligatory: sincere devotion to the Messenger by loving what He loves and hating what He hates. وَإِنْ تَظاهَرا عَلَيْهِ ** (But if you back up each other against him) And if you back up each other against him with that which displeases him. It was also recited with a lightened (التخفيف) form (تظاهرا).

In al-Majma' and al-Amali, narrated from Ibn 'Abbas: He asked 'Umar ibn al-Khattab who the two women were who backed up each other against the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. He replied: 'A'ishah and Hafsah.

In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Kazim عليه السلام: He recited: وَإِنْ تَظاهَرُوا عَلَيْهِ (And if you all back up each other against him).

I say: It is as if He included their fathers (أبويهما) with them. فَإِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ مَوْلاهُ وَجِبْرِيلُ وَصالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (Allah is indeed his Guardian, and Jibril, and the righteous among the faithful) Then he will not lack someone to back him up, for Allah is His Helper, and Jibril, the chief of the Cherubim (الكرّوبيّين), is his companion, and 'Ali ibn Abi Talib is his brother, his minister, and his soul (نفسه). وَالْمَلائِكَةُ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ ظَهِيرٌ (and the angels, after that, are his supporters)—they are those who back him up (مظاهرون).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, who said: **صالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (the righteous among the faithful) is 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام.

In al-Majma', narrated from him عليه السلام, he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم introduced 'Ali to his companions twice. Once was when He said: "Of whomsoever I am the Mawla (مولاه), 'Ali is his Mawla (مولاه)." And the second was when this Ayah was revealed: فَإِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ مَوْلاهُ وَجِبْرِيلُ وَصالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (Allah is indeed his Guardian, and Jibril, and the righteous among the faithful). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم took 'Ali's hand عليه السلام and said: "O people, this is the righteous among the faithful (صالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ)." And Asma' bint 'Umays said: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم says: وَصالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (and the righteous among the faithful) is 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام.

He said: And the narration has been transmitted through both the general (العام) and the specific (الخاص) routes that the intended meaning of صالِحُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (the righteous among the faithful) is 'Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام.

(٥) عَسى رَبُّهُ إِنْ طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَنْ يُبْدِلَهُ ** (5) Maybe, should He divorce you, his Lord will give Him in exchange وقرئ بالتخفيف أَزْواجاً خَيْراً مِنْكُنَّ مُسْلِماتٍ مُؤْمِناتٍ قانِتاتٍ تائِباتٍ عابِداتٍ سائِحاتٍ ** And it was read with takhfif (بالتخفيف) wives better than you, submissive [to Allah], faithful, obedient, penitent, devout, given to fasting, صائمات ثَيِّباتٍ وَأَبْكاراً ** meaning those who fast (صائمات) widows and virgins. وسط العاطف بينهما لتنافيهما ولأنّهما في حكم صفة واحدة إذ المعنى مشتملات على الثيّبات والأبكار. The conjunction (العاطف) was placed between the two because of their mutual exclusivity (لتنافيهما), and because they are in the ruling (حكم) of a single attribute (صفة واحدة), since the meaning is: encompassing (مشتملات) both widows (الثيّبات) and virgins (الأبكار).

(٦) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ ** (6) O you who have faith, save yourselves (by abandoning acts of disobedience and performing acts of obedience) and your families (by advising and disciplining) ناراً وَقُودُهَا النَّاسُ وَالْحِجارَةُ عَلَيْها مَلائِكَةٌ ** a Fire whose fuel is people and stones. Over it are angels (who manage its affair, and they are the Zabāniyah) غِلاظٌ شِدادٌ لا يَعْصُونَ اللهَ ما أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ. stern and severe, who do not disobey Allah in what He has commanded them, and carry out what they are commanded.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When this verse was revealed, a man from the Muslims sat weeping and said: "I am incapable regarding myself, yet I am tasked with my family!" Thereupon, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "It is sufficient for you that you command them with what you command yourself, and forbid them from what you forbid yourself."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "This is my soul (نفسي); I protect it. But how do I protect my family?" He said: "You command them with what Allah has commanded them, and forbid them from what Allah has forbidden them. If they obey you, then you have protected them. And if they disobey you, then you have fulfilled what is incumbent upon you."

And in al-Kafi: That which is close to it (in meaning).

(٧)** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لا تَعْتَذِرُوا الْيَوْمَ إِنَّما تُجْزَوْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** (7) O you who have disbelieved! Do not make excuses today. You are only being requited for what you used to do.

Meaning, that will be said to them upon their entry into the Fire. The prohibition against making excuses (الاعتذار) is either because there is no excuse (عذر) for them, or because the excuse will not benefit them.

(٨) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا تُوبُوا إِلَى اللهِ تَوْبَةً نَصُوحاً (8) O you who have faith! Repent to Allah with sincere repentance!

[It is] reaching the utmost degree of sincerity (النصح), and it is a characteristic of the penitent (التائب), for he advises (يَنْصَحُ) himself through repentance. It is described by this [adjective] based on metaphorical attribution (الاسناد المجازيّ) for emphasis (مبالغة). And it was recited with a dammah on the nūn (نُصُوحاً), and it is the verbal noun (المصدر).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "The servant repents from the sin, then he does not return to it." And in a narration: He was asked: "And which of us does not return [to sin]?" He said: "Indeed, Allah loves among His servants the one who is repeatedly tested (المفتن) [but is] constantly repentant (التوّاب)."

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "The servant repents, then he does not return to it. And the most beloved of Allah's servants to Allah is the one who is repeatedly tested (المفتن) [but is] the penitent (التائب)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): [A narration] with the same meaning.

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Sincere repentance (التوبة النصوح) is that the inner state (باطن) of the man is like his outer state (ظاهره), or better."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "When the servant repents with sincere repentance (توبة نصوحاً), Allah loves him, and He covers [his sins] for him in this world and the Hereafter." It was asked: "And how does He cover [his sins] for him?" He said: "He makes the two angels (ملكيه) forget what they wrote down of his sins, and He reveals to his limbs (جوارحه): 'Conceal his sins,' and He reveals to the regions of the earth (بقاع الأرض): 'Conceal what he used to do upon you of sins.' So, he meets Allah when he meets Him, and nothing of the sins testifies against him."

عَسى رَبُّكُمْ أَنْ يُكَفِّرَ عَنْكُمْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ وَيُدْخِلَكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ (Perhaps your Lord will absolve you of your misdeeds and admit you into gardens with streams running in them)

It was said: [The verse] is mentioned in the form of hopeful expectation (الاطماع), following the custom of kings, and indicating that it is a favor (تفضّل) and that repentance does not necessitate [reward] (غير موجب), and that the servant ought to be between fear (خوف) and hope (رجاء).

يَوْمَ لا يُخْزِي اللهُ النَّبِيَّ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ نُورُهُمْ يَسْعى بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمانِهِمْ. (on the Day when Allah will not disgrace the Prophet and those who have faith with him. Their light will move swiftly before them and on their right hand.)

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, He said: "The Imams (أئمّة) of the believers will move swiftly on the Day of Resurrection before the believers and on their right hands until they settle them in their stations (منازلهم) in Paradise." And al-Qummi narrated from Him (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to it.

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "So whoever has light on that Day will be saved, and every believer has light."

يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنا أَتْمِمْ لَنا نُورَنا وَاغْفِرْ لَنا إِنَّكَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (They will say, 'Our Lord! Perfect our light for us, and forgive us. Indeed, You have power over all things.')

(٩) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ. (9) O Prophet! Wage jihad against the faithless and the hypocrites,

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He recited: جاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ بالمنافقين (Wage jihad against the disbelievers with the hypocrites). He said: Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله never fought a hypocrite (منافقاً) at all; rather, He used to conciliate them (يتألّفهم).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: جاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ ** He said: "Thus was it revealed." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله waged jihad against the disbelievers (الكفّار), and Ali (peace be upon him) waged jihad against the hypocrites (المنافقين). Thus, Ali (peace be upon him) waged jihad like the jihad of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. And the complete explanation of this has preceded in Surah At-Tawbah. وَاغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ وَمَأْواهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ.** (And be severe with them. Their refuge is hell, and it is an evil destination.)

(١٠) ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا امْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَامْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ كانَتا تَحْتَ عَبْدَيْنِ مِنْ عِبادِنا صالِحَيْنِ فَخانَتاهُما ** (10) Allah cites an example for the faithless: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of Our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them. through hypocrisy (بالنفاق) and supporting [others] against the two Messengers (الرسولين). Allah likened the state of the disbelievers (الكفّار) and the hypocrites (المنافقين) in that they will be punished for their disbelief (كفرهم) and hypocrisy (نفاقهم), and they will not be favored (يحابون) due to the relationship (النّسبة) and connection (المواصلة) that exists between them and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the believers (المؤمنين), to the state of the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. And in this, there is an allusion (تعريض) to 'A'ishah and Hafsah regarding their betrayal (خيانتهما) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله by divulging His secret (إفشاء سرّه), their hypocrisy (نفاقهما) towards Him, and their supporting [each other] against Him, just as the wives of the two Messengers (الرسولين) did. فَلَمْ يُغْنِيا عَنْهُما مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً ** So they were not of any avail to them against Allah. Thus, the two Messengers (الرسولان) will not avail them in any way due to the right of marriage (بحقّ الزواج). وَقِيلَ ** and they were told, to them at their death or on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة): ادْخُلَا النَّارَ مَعَ الدَّاخِلِينَ ** 'Enter the Fire along with those who enter.' those who have no connection (وصلة) between them and the Prophets (الأنبياء).

(١١) وَضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا امْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (11) Allah has cited an example for those who have faith: Pharaoh’s wife, And [He cited] the example of the state of the believers, in that the connection (وصلة) with the disbelievers does not harm them in any way. [This refers to] Asiyah and her status with Allah, despite the fact that she was under the most hostile of Allah's enemies, إِذْ قالَتْ رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِنْدَكَ بَيْتاً فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَنَجِّنِي مِنْ فِرْعَوْنَ وَعَمَلِهِ when she said, 'My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise, and deliver me from Pharaoh and his conduct, [Meaning] from his wicked soul and his evil deed, وَنَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** and deliver me from the wrongdoing lot.' [Meaning] from the Copts (القبط) who followed him in wrongdoing (الظلم).

(12) ** وَمَرْيَمَ ابْنَتَ عِمْرانَ الَّتِي أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَها. (And Mary, daughter of 'Imran, who guarded her chastity.) Al-Qummi said: He did not look at her. فَنَفَخْنا فِيهِ ** (Then We breathed into it) meaning: into her private part. مِنْ رُوحِنا ** (of Our Spirit) meaning: from a spirit We created without the mediation of an origin (أصل). And Al-Qummi (said): meaning a created spirit (روح مخلوقة). وَصَدَّقَتْ بِكَلِماتِ رَبِّها وَكُتُبِهِ ** (and confirmed the words of her Lord and His scriptures). And it was read (وقرئ) as بِكِتابِهِ (with His scripture, singular). وَكانَتْ مِنَ الْقانِتِينَ ** (and was one of the devout) meaning: from those who persevere in obedience (المواظبين على الطاعة). And Al-Qummi (said): (meaning) from the supplicants (الدّاعين). The masculine gender (التذكير) is used for predominance (للتغليب) and to indicate that her obedience did not fall short of the obedience of the perfect men, such that she was counted among their number.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he said: "Many men reached perfection, but none of the women reached perfection except four: Asiyah bint Muzahim, the wife of Pharaoh; Maryam bint 'Imran; Khadijah bint Khuwaylid; and Fatimah bint Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله."

In al-Khisal, narrated from him عليه السلام: "The best women of the people of Paradise are Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, Fatimah عليها السلام bint Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, Maryam bint 'Imran, and Asiyah bint Muzahim, the wife of Pharaoh."

In al-Faqih: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله entered upon Khadijah while she was in her final illness (وهي لما بها). He said to her: "Despite us, what we see in you, O Khadijah. So when you arrive among your co-wives (ضرائرك), convey greetings (السلام) to them." She asked: "Who are they, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Maryam bint 'Imran, Kulthum (كلثم) the sister of Moses عليه السلام, and Asiyah the wife of Pharaoh." She said: "May it be with harmony (بالرّفاء), O Messenger of Allah. The reward for reading (the greetings) has already preceded."
